hero_description: KubeDB Operator extends Kubernetes with database-aware automation, enabling teams to run production databases using declarative APIs.
benefits_subtitle: Eliminate manual database operations with Kubernetes-native automation

benefits.title: 25+ Database Engines
benefits.description: Support for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, Kafka, and 25+ more databases, all managed through a unified platform.

benefits.title: Production-Grade Security
benefits.description: Built-in TLS/SSL encryption, RBAC integration, network policies, and security best practices enforced by default.

benefits.title: High Availability
benefits.description: Automatic failover, replica management, and self-healing capabilities ensure your databases stay online 24/7.

benefits.title: Automated Backup & Recovery
benefits.description: Scheduled backups with point-in-time recovery using Stash Enterprise. Restore databases to any point within retention period.

benefits.title: Performance Monitoring
benefits.description: Built-in Prometheus metrics, custom dashboards, and alerting to keep your databases performing at peak efficiency.

benefits.title: Multi-Cloud & Hybrid
benefits.description: Run on AWS, GCP, Azure, on-premises, or edge locations. One platform works everywhere Kubernetes runs.

benefits.title: Air-Gapped Deployments
benefits.description: Full support for isolated, air-gapped environments with private registries and no external dependencies.

benefits.title: Vertical & Horizontal Scaling
benefits.description: Scale up resources or add replicas dynamically without downtime. Handle growing workloads effortlessly.

benefits.title: GitOps Ready
benefits.description: Full declarative configuration via YAML manifests. Integrate seamlessly with ArgoCD, Flux, and other GitOps tools.

comparison_title: Operator vs Platform: What's the Difference?
comparison_subtitle: KubeDB Operator provides database automation inside a single cluster, while KubeDB Platform extends this into a centralized DBaaS across clusters, teams and environments.
comparison_analogy: The Operator is the engine. The Platform is the car.
comparison_analogy_explanation: The Platform provides centralized control and governance, orchestrating multiple Operator instances across different clusters and environments.

comparison_summary_cards.title: KubeDB Operator
comparison_summary_cards.description: Best for teams that want to run and automate databases inside a Kubernetes cluster using declarative APIs.

comparison_summary_cards.title: KubeDB Platform
comparison_summary_cards.description: Designed for organizations that need to deliver a standardized, self-service DBaaS across multiple clusters, teams, and environments with centralized control and governance.

rows: Use as KubeDB or internal tool

faqs_subtitle: Everything you need to know about KubeDB Operator

faqs.question: What's the difference between KubeDB Operator and KubeDB Platform?
faqs.answer: KubeDB Operator runs and automates databases inside a Kubernetes cluster. KubeDB Platform builds on the Operator to deliver a centralized, multi-cluster DBaaS with governance, self-service, and visibility across teams and environments.

faqs.question: Is KubeDB Platform priced separately from the Operator?
faqs.answer: No. KubeDB provides a unified, all-inclusive platform. The Operator and Platform capabilities are part of the same solution, allowing you to start small and scale into a full DBaaS without changing products or licensing.

faqs.question: Should I choose the Operator or the Platform for KubeDB?
faqs.answer: Start with Operator if: You're running databases in a single cluster or you want Kubernetes-native automation. Choose Platform if: You need DBaaS across teams or clusters, you require governance, RBAC, and visibility, or you are building an internal platform or managed service.

faqs.question: Where can KubeDB be deployed?
faqs.answer: KubeDB runs on any Kubernetes distribution, including on-premises, public cloud, hybrid environments, and air-gapped deployments.

faqs.question: How is KubeDB different from cloud-managed DBaaS services?
faqs.answer: Unlike cloud DBaaS, KubeDB runs inside Kubernetes, giving you full control over infrastructure, data placement, and operations across environments without vendor lock-in.

faqs.question: What databases does KubeDB support?
faqs.answer: KubeDB supports a wide range of databases including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, Elasticsearch, Cassandra, ClickHouse, and more.

faqs.question: Do I need KubeDB Platform to use KubeDB?
faqs.answer: No. You can start with the Operator to run databases in a single cluster. The Platform becomes valuable when you need centralized management, multi-cluster visibility, and a full DBaaS experience.

faqs.question: Does KubeDB support multi-tenancy?
faqs.answer: Yes. KubeDB Platform includes role-based access control, namespace isolation, and policy-driven governance to support multi-team and multi-tenant environments.

faqs.question: Does KubeDB provide backup and recovery?
faqs.answer: Yes. KubeDB includes database-aware, application-consistent backup and recovery integrated directly into database lifecycle management.

faqs.question: Can I move databases across environments with KubeDB?
faqs.answer: Yes. KubeDB enables consistent database operations across clusters and environments, making it easier to migrate workloads across cloud, on-prem, and hybrid deployments.
